stories.title: Kostyuk Stuns Swiatek 7-5, 6-1 on Her 25th Birthday

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: Kostyuk def. Swiatek 7-5, 6-1 at Roland Garros R4. On her 25th birthday. First set Kostyuk has ever won against her. The three-time defending champion's earliest exit at RG since 2019. #RolandGarros #Tennis

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Marta Kostyuk is 16-0 on clay in 2026. Titles in Rouen and Madrid. Now in her first Roland Garros QF. She just ended Swiatek's reign here 7-5, 6-1. Only Justine Henin (2005) matched this clay start. #RolandGarros #Tennis

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 8:00 AM ET

Happy birthday, Iga Swiatek? Not quite.

On her 25th birthday, the three-time defending Roland Garros champion walked off Court Philippe-Chatrier in one of the biggest upsets of the tournament's recent history. Marta Kostyuk — No. 15 seed, Ukrainian, absolutely on fire in 2026 — beat Swiatek 7-5, 6-1 to reach her first Roland Garros quarterfinal.

Let that sink in: Kostyuk had never beaten Swiatek in four previous meetings. She had never even taken a SET off her before today. On Sunday, she took both and won the match convincingly.

In 2026, Kostyuk is 16-0 on clay. She won titles in Rouen and Madrid. She just ended the most dominant player on the surface's French Open run. Only Justine Henin in 2005 has matched that kind of unbeaten clay start to a season since WTA rankings began.

Swiatek's earliest exit at Roland Garros since 2019. The draw just blew wide open.

Who do you think wins the women's title now?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 8:00 AM ET

Swiatek out. Gauff out. The Queen of Clay has left the building. Marta Kostyuk 7-5, 6-1 on her 25th birthday. #RolandGarros

article.headline: Birthday Shock: Kostyuk Stuns Swiatek 7-5, 6-1 to Reach First Roland Garros Quarterfinal

stories.title: Potapova Ends Gauff's Reign — Both Defending Champions Out Before Second Week

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: Potapova def. defending champion Gauff 4-6, 7-6(1), 6-4. Both Sinner and Gauff — the two Roland Garros defending champions — out before the second week. First time since 2004. #RolandGarros #Tennis

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: Roland Garros 2026: No Sinner. No Djokovic. No Alcaraz. No Swiatek. No Gauff. The defending men's and women's champions are both gone. The draw is as open as it has been in decades. #RolandGarros #Tennis

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 10:30 AM ET

History made at Roland Garros — and not in a good way for the defending champions.

Anastasia Potapova beat Coco Gauff 4-6, 7-6(1), 6-4 on Saturday in the third round. With Jannik Sinner also eliminated in round two, Roland Garros 2026 has become the first Grand Slam since 2004 to see both defending men's and women's singles champions exit before the second week.

Potapova was outstanding — 29 winners, 16 off that backhand. Gauff fought hard, but couldn't hold the tiebreak and couldn't recover.

It's a strange Roland Garros. Sinner out. Djokovic out. Alcaraz withdrew before the tournament. Swiatek just lost. Gauff just lost.

The second week is going to belong to someone unexpected. Who do you think it is?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 10:30 AM ET

The defending champion is out. Potapova beat Gauff 4-6, 7-6(1), 6-4. For the first time since 2004, both defending RG champions are gone before the second week. Tennis is unpredictable. #RolandGarros

stories.title: Sabalenka's 100th Win as No. 1 Sets Up Osaka Blockbuster in R16

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: Sabalenka def. Kasatkina 6-0, 7-5 for her 100th win as world No. 1. Next: Naomi Osaka (first RG second week since 2019) in R16. They met on clay in Madrid five weeks ago — Sabalenka won in three sets. #RolandGarros #Tennis

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 3:00 PM ET

Aryna Sabalenka hit a milestone she might not have even noticed in the moment: her 100th win as world No. 1.

She beat Daria Kasatkina 6-0, 7-5 on Saturday to reach the fourth round, where she'll face Naomi Osaka. Yes — Osaka is back. She beat Iva Jovic 7-6, 6-7, 6-4 in nearly three hours to reach the Roland Garros second week for the first time since 2019.

Sabalenka beat Osaka on clay in Madrid five weeks ago. This rematch is on the biggest stage. And with Swiatek now out, Sabalenka is the clear title favourite.

Are you on Team Sabalenka or Team Osaka for this R16 clash?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 3:00 PM ET

100 wins as world No. 1. Aryna Sabalenka is on a mission at Roland Garros. Next up: Naomi Osaka in the round of 16. #RolandGarros

stories.title: Open Draw — Who Can Win Roland Garros Now?

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: Women's Roland Garros QF contenders: Sabalenka (No. 1), Andreeva (16 clay wins), Potapova (def. defending champ Gauff), Kostyuk (16-0 on clay). No Swiatek. No Gauff. Wide open. #RolandGarros #Tennis

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 6:00 PM ET

Let's take a breath and look at what Roland Garros 2026 has become.

Women's side: Swiatek out. Gauff out. Rybakina out. The quarterfinal race belongs to Sabalenka, Kostyuk (16-0 on clay this year), Andreeva (32 wins on tour this season), and Potapova — who just beat the defending champion.

Men's side: Sinner out. Djokovic out. Alcaraz withdrew. Just two of the top eight seeds remain. Zverev is the favourite, but Fonseca (19 years old, beat Djokovic from two sets down) and Jódar (also 19, also in the second week) are writing the story of the tournament.

It's one of the most open Roland Garros draws in recent memory.

Which match are you most excited to watch this week?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 6:00 PM ET

Sinner out. Djokovic out. Alcaraz out. Swiatek out. Gauff out. Roland Garros 2026 is genuinely wide open. #RolandGarros #Tennis

stories.title: Fonseca vs. Ruud — Night Session Headline at Roland Garros

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: Fonseca vs. Ruud is tonight's night session at Philippe-Chatrier. The 19yo Brazilian who beat Djokovic in 5 sets is one win from the Roland Garros QF. No Sinner. No Djokovic. No Alcaraz. This tournament is wide open. #RolandGarros #Tennis

facebook.long_form:
**Window:** 9:00 PM ET

If you're watching one match tonight, make it Joao Fonseca vs. Casper Ruud on Philippe-Chatrier.

Fonseca is 19 years old, from Brazil, and three weeks ago most casual tennis fans couldn't have named him. Now? He's beaten Novak Djokovic from two sets down in nearly five hours, and tonight he's one win from the Roland Garros quarterfinals.

Ruud is not to be overlooked. He's been in two Roland Garros finals (2022 and 2023). He knows this court. He knows how to win in Paris. But that's what makes this match so compelling — the teenager on the run of his life vs. the veteran who has been here before.

Night session, Philippe-Chatrier. Don't miss it.

Who are you rooting for — Fonseca or Ruud?

---
facebook.image_caption:
**Window:** 9:00 PM ET

Fonseca vs. Ruud. Night session. Philippe-Chatrier. 19-year-old dream run vs. two-time finalist. Don't miss this one. #RolandGarros
